Walkthrough Standard batch 5 — 33 games updated [checklist passed]

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(sync): use SET LOCAL for RLS context without transaction

PROBLEM:
- RLS context verification failing with "expected X, got undefined"
- Used set_config('app.current_tenant_id', tenantId, true)
- Third param 'true' makes setting transaction-scoped
- No explicit BEGIN/COMMIT, so setting didn't persist

ROOT CAUSE:
set_config(..., true) requires active transaction context.
Without BEGIN, the setting is not reliably available for
subsequent queries in the same connection.

SOLUTION:
Changed to SET LOCAL app.current_tenant_id = $1
- Connection-scoped instead of transaction-scoped
- Works correctly outside explicit transactions
- Automatically cleared when connection returned to pool

REFERENCE:
- 001_tenant_context_function.sql documents both approaches
- SET LOCAL is safe for connection pooling (cleared on release)
- Neon serverless compatible

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: Only return active (unresolved) problems from Zabbix

The Zabbix proxy was using "recent": True which returns recent problems
including resolved ones. This caused the poller to report alerts that
had already been resolved.

Changes:
- Changed "recent": True to "recent": False
- Added "suppressed": False to exclude suppressed problems
- By default, problem.get returns only unresolved problems

Now the proxy will only return currently active problems that need attention.

feat: harden webhook server startup with exponential backoff and health CLI (#425)

- Add exponential backoff (500ms base, 30s cap, jitter) with 5 attempts on transient bind errors (EADDRINUSE) before falling back to polling
- Add `WebhookStatus` struct persisted to `~/.orch/webhook_status.json` tracking: configured, port, healthy, fallback_mode, last_check_utc, last_failure_reason, startup_attempts
- Add `orch webhook status` CLI subcommand that reads the persisted state for operator inspection
- Emit structured tracing fields `orch_webhook_up` and `orch_webhook_in_fallback` on health transitions
- Update `check_webhook_health()` to return `(bool, Option<String>)` with failure reason, which is persisted and cleared on recovery
- Add unit tests for `is_transient_bind_error`, `webhook_backoff_delay` cap, and EADDRINUSE integration test using real port binding

Co-authored-by: orchestrator[bot] <orchestrator@orchestrator.bot>

commit.message:
Fix crisis level assessment for negative-threshold indicators

The assessIndicatorLevel function used value <= max universally, but for
indicators with decreasing thresholds (e.g. Swap Spread: -10, -20, -30),
this caused all values to fall through to "extreme". Now detects threshold
direction and uses >= comparison for decreasing thresholds.

commit.message:
feat(web): surface intelligence reasoning in autopilot UI (#25)

* feat(autopilot): pass force=true on manual sync to recycle diagnoses

When users click "Check for updates" in the autopilot UI, pass
?force=true to sync and run-diagnosis endpoints so previously
executed/expired diagnoses are recycled back to PENDING immediately.

The 6h cooldown still applies for automated scheduler runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(autopilot): upgrade intelligence from rules to context-aware system

Phase 1: Wire up anomaly detection, budget optimizer, creative decay,
and rule tuner auto-adjust into diagnosis pipeline.

Phase 2: Add cross-data enrichment — funnel validation, unit economics
guard, cohort LTV context, and cross-channel awareness.

Phase 3: Add dynamic thresholds (percentile-based), trend acceleration
rule (Rule 13), predictive ROAS decline rule (Rule 14), and campaign
health scoring.

Phase 4: Enrich AI prompts with full business context (KPIs, funnel,
cohort, segments), add copy generator learning from past variants,
and connect suggestion feedback loop to diagnosis confidence.

Phase 5: Add Holt-Winters demand-aware budget planning with seasonal
multipliers — declining forecast penalizes budget increases, growing
forecast boosts and multiplies suggested budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(web): surface intelligence reasoning in autopilot UI

Add three user-facing components that expose backend intelligence:

1. ReasoningPills — expandable action cards now show why a recommendation
   was made: forecast trend, anomaly detection, funnel validation, unit
   economics, LTV context, channel mix, campaign health, creative decay,
   and seasonal context as colored pill rows.

2. ConfidenceBreakdown — horizontal bar showing the confidence score
   with a breakdown of what boosted or reduced it (anomaly +10,
   portfolio disagrees -20, etc).

3. ForecastWidget — overview tab now shows a revenue forecast trend
   card ("Trending up 12% — $12.4K projected next 7 days") using the
   existing Holt-Winters forecast endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add average_distinct metric type (#20832)

* feat: add average_distinct metric type

Add a new `average_distinct` metric type that computes the average of
values deduplicated by distinct keys, using the same CTE-based
ROW_NUMBER pattern as `sum_distinct`. Uses SUM/NULLIF(COUNT,0) with
CAST to floating type to prevent integer division on Postgres/Redshift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: include average_distinct in CTE path and rename sd -> dd

The CTE entry point (line 3019) only checked for SUM_DISTINCT, so
average_distinct metrics were excluded from normal SELECT but never
entered the CTE path, producing an empty query.

Also renames sd (sum_distinct) prefix to dd (deduplicated distinct)
across variables, CTE names, and SQL aliases to reflect that the
infrastructure now serves multiple metric types.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add NULL coverage to average_distinct e2e test

Add orders without payments to seed data (IDs 149-151) to produce
NULL amounts via the LEFT JOIN. Fix the test to properly extract
raw values from the v2 query API response envelope, and add a test
that verifies average_distinct returns NULL when all values are NULL.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: update average_distinct unit test for NULL-safe COUNT

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip NULLs in average_distinct COUNT to match SUM behavior

Change COUNT(CASE WHEN __dd_rn = 1 THEN 1 END) to
COUNT(CASE WHEN __dd_rn = 1 THEN __dd_val END) so that NULL values
are excluded from the denominator, matching how SUM skips them in
the numerator.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove COALESCE from sum_distinct to align with SQL NULL semantics

Both sum_distinct and average_distinct now return NULL for empty sets,
matching standard SQL SUM/AVG behavior instead of coercing to 0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(worktree): add cost tracking, session resume, and skills injection

Three patterns extracted from Paperclip (paperclipai/paperclip) adapter:

1. Cost parsing — new parse-session.sh extracts cost_usd, token usage,
   and session_id from Claude stream-json output. Saves per-worker cost
   data to .worktree-shared/costs/ for tracking.

2. Session resume — saves session_id after each run, automatically
   passes --resume on correction/retry tasks so workers don't lose
   context between rounds.

3. Skills injection — symlinks repo-local and global ~/.claude/skills/
   into a temp dir, passes via --add-dir so workers discover skills
   natively. Closes the "27 skills → 0" gap.

Also fixes --max-turns not being passed to claude CLI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
